spouse.name: Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II

zh.lifeSummary: 當Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel於大約1184年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯阿爾艾喬也斯時，她的父親Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I31歲，母親Alix d’Apchier31歲。她於1210年與Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1210年在她的家鄉享年27歲。

zh-hans.Châteauneuf-de-Randon, Lozère, Languedoc: 法国藍多-盧希隆洛艾a奧-德-藍登
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel于大约1184年出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯阿爾艾喬也斯时，她的父亲Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I31岁，母亲Alix d’Apchier31岁。她于1210年与Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1210年在她的家乡享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guerin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Marcel, Normand, Andre, Dominique, Aime, Alain, Arsene, Benoit, Francois, Gilles.

French (Guérin) and Irish (of French Huguenot origin): from the ancient Germanic personal name Warino, Latinized as Guarinus, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France, partly as a result of the popularity of the Carolingian lay Guérin de Montglave. This was the name of several Huguenot families who came to England as refugees at different times in the 16th and 17th centuries. The surname became established as a surname in Ireland (County Limerick) as early as the 16th century. Compare Garin , Garing , Gearing , Geren , Geron , Gerren , Geurin , Guerrant , and Ward .
Italian (Friuli Venezia Giulia): variant of   Guerino  .

variants.name: Guerrant
meanings: Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).

In some cases possibly also French (Seine-Maritime): variant of Guerrand, a nickname for a belligerent person or alternatively for a valiant soldier, derived from guerrer ‘to fight’. The surname Guerrant is very rare in France.

History: Daniel Guérin/Guerrant from Saint-Nazaire-sur-Charente in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie L'Orange in Saint-Nazaire in 1697, died in Goochland, VA, in 1721. His name is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Gearing
meanings: English (Sussex and London): perhaps a nickname from Middle English gering, apparently with the sense ‘lecher; glutton’.
English (of French Huguenot origin): Anglicized form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
Americanized form of German   Gehring   or of some other similar (like-sounding) German surname (compare e.g.   Gahring   and   Garing  ).

variants.name: Geren
meanings: Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
In some cases possibly also an altered form of German Gehren, a variant of Gehr 1. The surname Gehren is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Garin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Benedicto, Beatriz, Consuelo, Dinora, Eduardo, Ines, Manuel, Marta, Noemi, Rodolfo, Rogelio, Simona.

Spanish (Garín) and French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Warino, Latinized as G(u)arinus, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare French Guerin .
Russian: patronymic from Garya, a short form of the personal name Garasim, a variant of Gerasim (see Gerasimov ).

variants.name: Gerren
meanings: Americanized form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).

variants.name: Geurin
meanings: Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Tournel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Turney  .

variants.name: Turney
meanings: English (of Norman origin):
habitational name from any of the places in northern France called Tournai-sur-Dive (Orne), Tournay-sur-Odon (Calvados), or Tourny (Eure), or occasionally from Tournai in Hainaut (Belgium).

perhaps occasionally an occupational name from Middle English tournei, turnai ‘lawyer’ (Anglo-Norman French tourné), or a nickname from Middle English tournei(en) ‘(to) joust, take part in a tournament’ (Old French torneier). Compare Turner .

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: When Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel was born about 1184, in Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, her father, Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I, was 31 and her mother, Alix d’Apchier, was 31. She married Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II in 1210. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1210, in her hometown, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel est née environ 1184, à Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Alix d’Apchier, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II en 1210. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1210, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel um 1184 in Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I 31 und ihre Mutter Alix d’Apchier 31. Sie hat 1210 Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1210 im Alter von 27 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel è nata il circa 1184 nel Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, sua padre, Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Alix d’Apchier, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II nel 1210. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1210, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes,: フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスアルドeチェジョイエウゼ
ja.Châteauneuf-de-Randon, Lozère, Occitanie: フランスオクシタニーロズeアチaテアウネウフ-デ-ランドン
ja.Altier, Lozère, Occitanie, France: フランスオクシタニーロズeアアルティエル
ja.Châteauneuf-de-Randon, Lozère, Languedoc: フランスラングエドク-ロウシロンロズeアチaテアウネウフ-デ-ランドン
ja.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Guerin du Tournelが約1184年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスアルドeチェジョイエウゼで生まれた時，父Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon Iは31歳，母Alix d’Apchierは31歳であった。1210年にOdilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1210年に故郷で27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel(이)가 대략 1184년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 아드e체 조예우스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alix d’Apchier(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1210년 Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1210년 그녀의 고향에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel nasceu aproximadamente 1184, em Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Alix d’Apchier, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II em 1210. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1210, em sua cidade natal, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel родилась около 1184 г. в местности джоиеус, ардeч, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Alix d’Apchier, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II в 1210 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1210 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marguerite de Guerin du Tournel nació aproximadamente 1184, en Joyeuse, Ardèche, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Guerin de Châteauneuf-de-Randon I, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Alix d’Apchier, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Odilon de Châteauneuf-Randon du Guérin II en 1210. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1210, en su pueblo, a la edad de 27 años.
